challenges.title: Shopping List
challenges.description: Create an unordered list (<ul>) with at least 4 list items (<li>) representing items on a shopping list. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Write your shopping list here -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '
    ' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a
      unordered list element' assert '
    ' in html.lower(), 'Should close the
      element' assert html.lower().count('
    • ') >= 4, 'Should contain at least 4
    • list items' assert html.lower().count('
      • ') >= 4, 'Should close all
    • elements' for i, item in enumerate(html.lower().split('
    • ')[1:5]): text = item.split('
      • ')[0].strip() assert len(text) > 0, f'List item {i+1} should contain some text'
hints: An unordered list uses <ul> as the container and <li> for each item

Each
  • should contain the name of a shopping item, like
  • Milk

challenges.title: Nested Lists
challenges.description: Create an ordered list (<ol>) with at least 3 items. At least one of those items should contain a nested unordered list (<ul>) with at least 2 sub-items. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Write an ordered list with a nested unordered list -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '
    ' in html.lower(), 'Should contain an
      ordered list element' assert '
    ' in html.lower(), 'Should close the
      element' assert html.lower().count('
    1. ') >= 5, 'Should contain at least 5
    2. items total (3 outer + 2 nested)' assert '
        ' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a nested
          unordered list' assert '
        ' in html.lower(), 'Should close the nested
          element' assert html.lower().index('
            ') < html.lower().index('
              '), 'The
                should appear before the nested
                  '
hints: Place a <ul> with its own <li> items inside one of the outer <li> elements
